motivation: Persist note tags in the backend and expose them through the notes API so the v2 UI's tag chips and tag filtering use real user data instead of empty placeholder arrays.

included: backend note tag persistence: SQLAlchemy model relationship plus Alembic migration for per-note tags
backend notes API contract: NoteResponse.tags, NoteCreate.tags, NoteUpdate.tags, and tag serialization on list/get/create/update
backend validation and ownership behavior for note tags
frontend notes API DTO and useNotes adapter update so tag UI consumes backend tags
tests for migration, backend notes tag API behavior, and frontend tag rendering/filtering
excluded: profile settings, workspace settings, existing settings-page UI wiring, and SSO settings TODOs
SSE note events; the backend SSE infrastructure is parked for future UI upgrades and is not part of this binder
server-side search or tag query parameters for GET /notes/; the current v2 UI filters client-side after list fetch
new visible tag-editing UI beyond the existing read/list/filter surfaces; this binder only makes backend create/update contracts ready and consumes returned tags
bulk tag management, tag rename/delete endpoints, colors, ordering controls, and cross-note tag administration

design_facts.stack: Mixed backend/frontend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy async + Alembic + PostgreSQL backend; Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite frontend with ofetch services and composable singleton state.

work_items.title: Add note tag table and ORM relationship

exposes.storage: A note_tags table that stores zero or more tags for a note. Each row belongs to one Note through note_id with ON DELETE CASCADE. Tags are stored as normalized text without a leading #. A note cannot have duplicate normalized tags.
exposes.orm: A Note.tags relationship that loads tag rows for a note and cascades deletes with the owning note.

validation.normalization: Trim whitespace, drop a single leading # if present, lowercase the value, collapse internal whitespace to hyphen, and reject empty tags after normalization.
validation.limits: Each tag is 1-64 characters after normalization. Tags may contain lowercase letters, numbers, hyphen, and underscore.
validation.tenant_boundary: Tags are reachable only through notes owned by the authenticated user; no cross-user tag lookup surface is added.

assertions: Alembic upgrade creates note_tags with note_id, tag, the note foreign key cascade, an index usable for tag lookup, and a uniqueness constraint preventing duplicate tags per note.
Alembic downgrade removes note_tags cleanly without changing existing notes/users tables.
Deleting a note deletes its tag rows through the declared relationship/cascade behavior.
The migration follows the repo's brownfield-tolerant guard convention for fresh and existing databases.
oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/migrations/test_note_tags_migration.py -v

exposes.schema: NoteResponse includes tags: list[str]. Existing notes with no tags return tags: [].
exposes.routes: GET /api/notes/ and GET /api/notes/{note_id} return tags for each note. POST and PUT responses also include tags after the write contract lands.

contract.ordering: Tags are returned in stable alphabetical order after normalization.
contract.performance: List responses avoid one query per note; use SQLAlchemy relationship loading or an equivalent batched query.

signals: blast-radius:notes list, note detail, frontend DTOs depend on this shape

assertions: GET /api/notes/ returns every note with a tags array, including [] for untagged notes.
GET /api/notes/{note_id} returns the selected note with its tags in stable normalized order.
A user never sees tags for another user's notes.
The OpenAPI schema for NoteResponse includes tags as an array of strings.
oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/notes/test_note_tags_api.py -v

work_items.title: Accept tags on create and update

exposes.create: NoteCreate accepts optional tags: list[str] | null. Omitted or null means create the note with no tags. A provided list is normalized, deduplicated, validated, stored, and returned on the created NoteResponse.
exposes.update: NoteUpdate accepts optional tags: list[str] | null. Omitted means leave existing tags unchanged. A provided list replaces the complete tag set; [] clears all tags.
exposes.response: POST /api/notes/ and PUT /api/notes/{note_id} return NoteResponse including the persisted tags.

contract.errors: Invalid tags return 422 validation errors with field-level detail from Pydantic/FastAPI. Updating another user's note still returns 404.
contract.backward_compatibility: Existing frontend/backend callers that omit tags keep working.

signals: capability-escalation:new write path for persisted tags

assertions: POST /api/notes/ with tags stores normalized, deduplicated tags and returns them on the response.
POST /api/notes/ without tags still succeeds and returns tags: [].
PUT /api/notes/{note_id} with tags replaces the full tag set; [] clears it.
PUT /api/notes/{note_id} without a tags key leaves existing tags unchanged.
Invalid tag values fail validation without creating or changing tag rows.
oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/notes/test_note_tags_api.py -v

work_items.title: Consume backend tags in the v2 UI

exposes.adapter: useNotes maps dto.tags into NoteMock.tags instead of forcing tags: []. Missing tags in defensive tests may map to [] only for test/backward tolerance, but the documented API contract expects tags to be present.
exposes.ui: Existing TagFilter, NoteCard tag chips, and active note metadata chips render real backend tags and keep using the existing client-side filter logic.

contract.tests: Re-enable the AppShell tag filter tests currently deferred for the backend tags gap, using NoteResponse fixtures that include tags.

signals: contract-mutation:frontend DTO shape consumes new backend field
blast-radius:AppShell tag filtering and existing tests change from empty placeholder arrays to real data

assertions: NoteDTO includes tags and service tests use NoteResponse payloads with tags.
fetchNotes() maps backend tags to NoteMock.tags instead of replacing them with [].
AppShell tag filter tests are active and pass against fetched NoteResponse fixtures with tags.
Existing search, read-pane, note-card, create-local-note, copy/export/delete-local-note tests continue to pass.
oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/notes.test.ts src/features/notes/useNotes.test.ts src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts

work_items.title: Run full backend and frontend gates for tags

contract.exposes: A final whole-repo verification point after backend schema/API and frontend DTO consumption land.
contract.coverage: Runs the same broad gates documented for this repo: UI lint/tests and backend lint/format/types/pytest. This item should make no feature changes beyond test or fixture corrections needed to keep the full gates honest.

assertions: The complete repo test target passes after the tag schema/API/UI changes.
No tag-related test is skipped or commented out because of the previous backend tags gap.
The final diff does not touch the parked SSE infrastructure.
